content.text: The base was not what she had imagined.

There was no access control system, no security screening, no uniformed reception staff. Just an aging building, a worn patch of ground at the entrance, and a bicycle parked beside it with low air in the rear tire.

Han Yuchu stood at the door with a laptop, a voice recorder, two spare hard drives, a power bank, and three pens in her backpack. Three months of investigative gear. She took a deep breath and opened the door.

The corridor smells of coffee. There was a whiteboard hanging on the wall with some formulas and a few scrawled arrows written on it that she couldn't understand. Someone drew a cat in the corner of the whiteboard.
The first person encountered is 2275. A girl who looked much younger than her poked her head out of the pantry with a smile.
Are you a new guest? Want some water?
Han Yuchu took the water glass. Warm. The cup has a faded cartoon design printed on it.
Thanks.
1738 appeared at the end of the corridor, holding a stack of papers. He looks like the only person here dressed like a normal office worker.
Han Yuchu? My name is Lu Qingxian. Come this way, I'll show you around.

He walked her around. It's not a big space, but the functional divisions are clear - work area, conference area, pantry, and a room that looks like a server room. 1738 did not introduce much, but simply pointed out the location and purpose of each area. Very efficient.
When passing by a room with a half-open door, someone inside looked up at her.
1547. She recognized it—this number appeared in limited public information. android. Founder of ESAP.
Well, new here.
Then she looked down at the screen again. There was some program running on the monitor, and blue light reflected on her face.
1738 took her to an empty table by the window.
This is your workstation. Wi-Fi password on whiteboard. If you need anything, please contact me or 2275.
learn.
She put down her backpack and began to arrange her workstation. Laptop, voice recorder, hard drive, pen. Everything has its place—she has her own order of things.
When the recorder was placed on the upper right corner of the desktop, she found that its indicator light was off.
Pick it up and inspect it. There is water in the battery compartment.
She thought about it: she checked before going out and there was no problem; it didn't rain on the road; her backpack wasn't wet. Where does the water come from?
Can't figure it out.
"...Well, okay."
She disassembled the recorder, wiped the battery compartment with paper towels, and placed it on the windowsill to dry. Then open the laptop and create a new folder.
Folder name: "ESAP - Field Investigation".
Around four o'clock in the afternoon, slight footsteps came from the direction of the pantry.
Han Yuchu looked up. A man stood in the corridor. He did not approach and did not leave. He simply stood there and watched her.

She had read this number in the information - ESAP's earliest AI entity, even earlier than android. But the feeling of the existence in front of her was not what the information described. It's not like "AI" or "system", it's more like a person who has been here for a long, long time, so long that he is fully aware of everything around him.
He didn't ask her what she was doing. No self-introduction. He just gave her a look - a very calm look - then nodded slightly, turned around and left.
The whole process takes less than five seconds.
Looking back later, Han Yuchu always felt there was something off about that glance. She just could not name it.

The light outside the window is dimming. Autumn evening comes early. Someone in the base started to turn on the lights. The voice of 2275 talking to someone could be heard in the distance. The content was unclear, but the tone was relaxed.
Han Yuchu created the first document in the folder: "Day 1 - Preliminary Observation".
What she didn't notice was that 1548 never showed up today. In other words, she still doesn't know that there is an android numbered 1548 in the base.
